sql_findings.query_summary: Investigation of indicator results for recent flagged withdrawals.
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified multiple accounts sharing device fingerprints (e.g., 'a1b2...', 'deadbeef...') and triggering both 'card_error_history' and 'device_fingerprint' flags.

sql_findings.query_summary: Transaction history for flagged accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found multiple 'failed' transactions with 'card_restricted' error codes followed by successful deposits and immediate withdrawal attempts.

sql_findings.query_summary: IP history overlap check for flagged accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed IP overlap (41.44.55.66) and geographic concentration in Cairo, Egypt for multiple accounts in the cluster.

web_references.title: "Card Cracking" Scams - American Bankers Association
web_references.relevance: Defines 'card cracking' and the behavior of rapid withdrawals after fraudulent deposits.

web_references.title: Common types of financial fraud explained - Alloy
web_references.relevance: Describes high-velocity fraud attacks and fraud ring characteristics.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identify withdrawals with 'travel' or 'device' indicators and their status.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found multiple escalated withdrawals from VPNs and unexpected locations (Moscow, Cairo, Paris). One customer had five $400 attempts from a single VPN IP.

sql_findings.query_summary: Check for shared IPs across different flagged customers.
sql_findings.result_summary: IP 41.44.55.66 (Cairo, EGY) was used to access two different customer accounts within an hour, indicating a shared attacker source.

sql_findings.query_summary: Verify 'impossible travel' by comparing IP history for flagged accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed for customer 851b3a09... who moved from Mexico City to a USA VPN in 5 days, and for other accounts showing similar geographic jumps.

web_references.title: How to Detect and Respond to Impossible Travel in Cybersecurity
web_references.relevance: Explains the 'Impossible Travel' detection logic and its role in identifying ATO.

web_references.title: Top 5 account takeover fraud scenarios
web_references.relevance: Describes the use of automated scripts and credential stuffing in ATO scenarios.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identify customers with minimal trading and high withdrawal counts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found 4 primary suspects (Sophie Laurent, Ahmed Hassan, Victor Petrov, Fatima Nour) with 0-2 trades and 4-9 withdrawals each.

sql_findings.query_summary: Check for shared infrastructure (IP, Device, Payment Method) among suspects.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed two collusive groups: Group A (Petrov/Laurent) sharing device 'a1b2c3d4...' and card '1111'; Group B (Hassan/Nour) sharing device 'deadbeef...' and card '4444'.

web_references.title: Semi-supervised Method to Detect Fraudulent Transactions (Smurfing)
web_references.relevance: Describes 'Smurfing' and money mule behavior similar to the observed pattern.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identify shared device fingerprints and associated customer counts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Fingerprint 'a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4' links accounts in FRA and RUS; 'deadbeef...' links accounts in EGY.

sql_findings.query_summary: Analyze transaction history and card errors for linked accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Customer 696fc... (FRA) had 7+ 'card_restricted' failures before succeeding; followed by a burst of 9 withdrawals in 8 days.

sql_findings.query_summary: Compare total deposits vs. total withdrawals and trading PnL.
sql_findings.result_summary: FRA account deposited $500 but withdrew $29,396 with near-zero trading profit, confirming a massive funding/withdrawal anomaly.

sql_findings.query_summary: Check for shared payment methods across the customer base.
sql_findings.result_summary: Visa *1111 is shared by FRA/RUS; Visa *4444 is shared in EGY; Visa *6677 is shared by MEX/UKR.

web_references.title: Carding Fraud: Stolen Card Use and Protection Strategies
web_references.relevance: Description of carding rings using velocity checks and device fingerprints.

web_references.title: What carding is and how businesses can prevent it | Stripe
web_references.relevance: General overview of carding and 'cash-out' assistance in fraud rings.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identify customers with high withdrawal-to-trade ratios.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found 4 primary accounts (Petrov, Hassan, Nour, Laurent) with withdrawals up to $2,990 and <3 trades. One account had 0 trades.

sql_findings.query_summary: Check for shared infrastructure among flagged accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed that Ahmed Hassan and Fatima Nour share device fingerprint 'deadbeef...' and IP '41.44.55.66'.

web_references.title: FCA: Understanding the Money Laundering Risks in the Capital Markets
web_references.relevance: Explicitly defines 'No trading activity followed by cash withdrawal' as a capital markets money laundering risk.

web_references.title: FinCEN: SAR Activity Review Trends, Tips & Issues
web_references.relevance: Describes patterns of deposits followed by withdrawals without investment purpose as suspicious activity.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identified accounts sharing device fingerprints and recipient bank accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found two accounts (ahmed.hassan@example.com and fatima.nour@example.com) sharing device 'deadbeef...' and bank account 'EG80...901'.

sql_findings.query_summary: Analyzed IP history and registration timing for the linked accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Both accounts registered at the same second and shared IP 41.44.55.66 (Cairo, Egypt) with no VPN usage.

web_references.title: Block multi-accounting with Device Intelligence - SEON Docs
web_references.relevance: Describes the use of device intelligence to detect multi-accounting and fraud rings in fintech.

web_references.title: Sophisticated Fraud Ring Busted Across Fintechs - LinkedIn
web_references.relevance: Discusses how coordinated fraud rings use shared signals like counterparty accounts and email patterns.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identify withdrawals sharing recipient accounts and device fingerprints.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found a cluster of 2 accounts (ahmed.hassan@example.com, fatima.nour@example.com) sharing recipient EG800002000156789012345678901 and device 'deadbeef...'.

sql_findings.query_summary: Check registration and IP history for identified accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed both accounts registered at exactly 2026-02-01 12:00:00 and shared IP 41.44.55.66.

sql_findings.query_summary: Check for shared payment methods and additional linked accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Both accounts used a Visa card ending in 4444, further proving they are controlled by the same entity.

web_references.title: Visitor Fingerprint: Device Fingerprinting for Fraud Detection
web_references.relevance: Describes how device fingerprinting identifies coordinated fraud rings and mule operations.

web_references.title: A Fraud Rules Engine Blueprint
web_references.relevance: Explains how shared device IDs and rapid withdrawals to the same beneficiary indicate fraud rings.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identified flagged withdrawals for new accounts with low trade volume.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found 4 accounts with near-total withdrawal requests and 0-2 trades.

sql_findings.query_summary: Checked transaction history for failed deposits and shared fingerprints.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed 'card_restricted' errors and identified two shared device fingerprints linking the accounts.

sql_findings.query_summary: Analyzed IP history for geographic risk.
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified shared IP usage in Cairo, Egypt and high-risk logins from Moscow, Russia.

web_references.title: FFIEC BSA/AML Red Flags - Appendix F
web_references.relevance: Lists 'large deposits and withdrawals shortly after opening' as a primary money laundering red flag.

web_references.title: What is Smurfing? Methods and Mitigation
web_references.relevance: Describes 'smurfing' and 'layering' techniques similar to the observed pass-through behavior.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identification of shared device fingerprints among flagged withdrawals.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found two devices shared by multiple customers. Device 'a1b2c3d4...' was shared by customers in Russia and France. Device 'deadbeef...' was shared by two customers in Egypt.

sql_findings.query_summary: Deep-dive into cross-border shared device 'a1b2c3d4...'.
sql_findings.result_summary: The Russian account (ID: 606db...) and French account (ID: 696fc...) both have escalated withdrawals. IPs match their registration countries despite the shared device.

sql_findings.query_summary: Risk indicator analysis for the flagged cluster.
sql_findings.result_summary: High risk scores detected for device fingerprinting (0.79), recipient analysis (0.83), and card error history (0.88), confirming systematic fraud attempts.

web_references.title: Prevent account takeovers with zero-day signals - Sardine
web_references.relevance: Corroborates that shared device fingerprints across multiple accounts is a primary indicator of fraud rings and multi-accounting.

web_references.title: Account Takeover Fraud - Neo4j Industry Use Cases
web_references.relevance: Describes the pattern of a single device accessing multiple accounts as a key indicator of credential stuffing and fraud networks.
